    "R1.1": "The section makes explicit contribution claims ('first O(1/√T) rate') and a gap statement (discrete selection resists standard projected-SGD). Multiple internal logical tensions require checking: (1) the paper frames the objective as non-convex hard-selection but the convergence proof operates on the relaxed convex simplex — the bridge between these is asserted but not argued; (2) the text simultaneously claims μ-strong convexity (which yields O(log(1/ε)) linear rates) and O(1/√T) sublinear rates without reconciling the two regimes; (3) the conclusion that 'hard selection imposes no additional rate penalties' does not follow from the relaxed-simplex analysis alone.",

The alignment between the stated assumptions (L-smooth, μ-strongly convex, bounded Hessian) and the eventual O(1/√T) headline rate — rather than the faster strongly-convex O(log(1/ε)) rate — needs scrutiny.",
    "R1.4": "The section explicitly claims a 'first' result. Assessing whether this delta is genuine requires comparing against Cesa-Bianchi and Lugosi (2006) and Bubeck (2015) as cited — the current framing conflates recovering known simplex rates with establishing new selection-specific rates.",

rationale.R1.1: The section makes explicit contribution claims ('first O(1/√T) rate') and a gap statement (discrete selection resists standard projected-SGD). These must be reconstructed and verified for precision — e.g., the claimed novelty is in tension with the simultaneous assertion that the rate 'matches the optimal rate' already in Cesa-Bianchi and Lugosi (2006).
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing. Multiple internal logical tensions require checking: (1) the paper frames the objective as non-convex hard-selection but the convergence proof operates on the relaxed convex simplex — the bridge between these is asserted but not argued; (2) the text simultaneously claims μ-strong convexity (which yields O(log(1/ε)) linear rates) and O(1/√T) sublinear rates without reconciling the two regimes; (3) the conclusion that 'hard selection imposes no additional rate penalties' does not follow from the relaxed-simplex analysis alone.
rationale.R1.3: The theoretical claims invoke Section 5 for empirical support (not present) and anchor rate claims on cited works. The alignment between the stated assumptions (L-smooth, μ-strongly convex, bounded Hessian) and the eventual O(1/√T) headline rate — rather than the faster strongly-convex O(log(1/ε)) rate — needs scrutiny.
rationale.R1.4: The section explicitly claims a 'first' result. Assessing whether this delta is genuine requires comparing against Cesa-Bianchi and Lugosi (2006) and Bubeck (2015) as cited — the current framing conflates recovering known simplex rates with establishing new selection-specific rates.
rationale.R1.7: Two specific attributions are made: Cesa-Bianchi and Lugosi (2006) for the O(log K / √T) optimal simplex rate and Bubeck (2015) for mirror-descent iteration complexity. Accuracy of these attributions and whether they support the claims as stated is in scope for a review.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review treat novelty assessment and citation accuracy as load-bearing (i.e., a full peer-style review where an incorrect novelty claim or misattributed rate would constitute a critical failure), or is the primary mandate limited to checking internal logical and mathematical consistency?
